title: Trump Holds Press Conference After WHCD Shooting, Calls Suspect a 'Lone Wolf Whack Job'
detail: President Trump held a press conference following the April 25 shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, calling suspect Cole Tomas Allen a 'lone wolf whack job' and a 'very sick person.' Trump said he was asking 'all Americans to recommit with their hearts to resolving our differences peacefully.' Acting AG Todd Blanche confirmed investigators believe Allen was targeting Trump administration officials, 'likely including the President.' Trump vowed to reschedule the Correspondents' Dinner and proposed building a large, secure ballroom on White House grounds. The incident has prompted renewed calls from both parties for improved security protocols at high-profile events attended by senior government officials.

media.caption: Trump at press conference following the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, April 26, 2026

title: USTR Sets April 28 Public Hearing on Section 301 Trade Investigations Against 16 Countries
detail: The Office of the United States Trade Representative announced a public hearing scheduled for April 28, 2026 on Section 301 trade investigations targeting 16 countries, including China, the EU, Japan, India, and Mexico. The investigations were launched after the Supreme Court's February 2026 ruling struck down IEEPA tariff authority; the administration is seeking new legal grounds for reciprocal tariffs before the 10% universal Section 122 surcharge expires on July 24, 2026. The investigations cover structural manufacturing overcapacity and trade practices that the administration says unfairly disadvantage US industries.

media.caption: USTR announces Section 301 investigation hearings on trade practices of 16 countries
